content.full_text:
CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
P.O. BOX 1768, NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92659-1768
(714) 644-3131

April 8, 1992

Ms. Deanne Stone, Esq.
Staff Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice;
Letter No. 92-180/Councilmember Evelyn Hart

Dear Ms. Stone:

This letter will supplement information you have already
received relative to the request of Councilmember Evelyn Hart for
formal written advice. As you know, the request pertains to the
ability of Councilmember Hart to participate in the process leading
to the adoption of a Specific Area Plan for a portion of her
district known as Old Newport Boulevard.

In 1970, Councilmember Hart and her husband purchased property
at 506 Orange. The property is a duplex on B-2 lot. The
property was purchased prior to Councilmember Hart's election in
1978. The units are rarely, if ever, vacant.

Old Newport Boulevard has been designated as a specific plan
area since 1973. On February 24, 1992, the City Council voted to
initiate a general plan amendment to designate a portion of the Old
Newport Boulevard Specific Plan Area for "mixed use" (commercial on
first floor and residential on second). The area is now zoned
strictly commercial. The Council also voted to consider expanding
or modifying the boundaries of the Specific Area Plan for Old
Newport Boulevard. The property owned by Councilmember Hart if in
an area with some residents want rezoned to a lower floor area
ratio (from 2.0 to 1.5). The property owned by Councilmember Hart
is more than 300 feet from the area designated for possible
amendment and the value of her property would be unaffected by any
such amendment. The Council also asked staff to prepare a
resolution establishing a Specific Area Plan Ad Hoc Committee and
a scope of services for an economic base study.

3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach

Deanne Stone, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 8, 1992
Page 2.

On March 23, 1992, the City Council approved, with
Councilmember Hart abstaining, a resolution establishing an Ad Hoc
Committee with the responsibility to "provide input and guidance
for preparation and adoption of the Old Newport Boulevard Specific
Area Plan." The Mayor postponed appointments to the Committee
pending your response to this request for advice. Ordinarily, the
Mayor would appoint Evelyn Hart as the Chairperson of the Ad Hoc
Committee since she is the Councilmember representing the district
in which the property is located.

The Ad Hoc Committee would typically hold a series of meetings
with business owners, property owners, affected residents and any
other interested parties to receive ideas as to appropriate land
use, development standards and public improvements for the area.
These meetings are generally open to the public with appropriate
notice given. Over time, the Committee would try to develop a
consensus on plan components and, with staff assistance, develop a
draft specific area plan. The draft plan would then be submitted
to the Planning Commission and City Council as a recommendation of
the Committee. In the past, the Planning Commission and City
Council have significantly modified draft plans submitted by Ad Hoc
Committees.

The City Council has approved a number of specific area plans.
These plans contain a number of components including permitted land
uses, specific development standards, preferred architectural
styles and themes, and proposed public improvements. The public
improvement components for other specific area plans have included
the improvement of streets and sidewalks, installation of
landscaping, installation of special theme street lighting, and
construction of public facilities such as restrooms. The public
improvement component of the Old Newport Boulevard Specific Area
Plan would probably not include construction of visitors serving
public facilities since the area is not frequented by tourists or
beach-goers.

Councilmember Hart's property is within 85 feet of the
boundaries of the Specific Area Plan. The property is
approximately 100 feet from the closest parcel within the Specific
Area Plan boundary. The property is located adjacent to the north
end of the Specific Area Plan boundary and within none of the parcels
within the Specific Plan Area front on Orange Avenue. The nine
parcels within the Specific Area Plan boundary that are closest to
the Hart property face Old Newport Boulevard. The vast majority of
the parcels within the Specific Plan Area are more than 300 feet
from the Hart residence. It is extremely unlikely that the public
facility component of the Old Newport Boulevard Specific Area Plan

Deanne Stone, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 8, 1992
Page 3.


would result in any significant improvement to Orange Avenue or
Clay Street (see map attached).  This office has no information
that would suggest adoption of a Specific Area Plan for Old Newport
Boulevard would have any affect on the value of the Hart property.

Councilmember Hart would like you to address the following
questions:

1.  Does her ownership of the property at 506 Orange Avenue
preclude her from accepting an appointment to the City Council
Ad Hoc Specific Area Plan Committee:

(a)  assuming the boundaries of the Specific Area Plan will
     not be modified to include the Hart property;
(b)  assuming the boundaries of the Specific Area Plan will be
     modified to include the Hart property;

2.  Assuming Councilmember Hart is precluded from membership on
the Specific Area Plan Committee, are there any constraints on
Councilmember Hart's ability to:

(a)  attend meetings as a member of the general public;
(b)  provide input during Committee meetings;
(c)  or, provide written comments to the Committee.

3.  In the absence of any indication as to the contents of the
Specific Area Plan, are there any criteria useful to
Councilmember Hart in determining whether a Specific Plan
would have any impact on the value of her property?

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    [signature]
                                    Robert H. Burnham
                                    City Attorney

RHB:kmc
Attachment
